title: Quality
point: Quality is the degree to which inherent characteristics fulfill requirements.

title: Inherent Characteristics
point: Quality is measured through inherent characteristics of a product or service.

title: Physical Characteristics
point: Physical characteristics include mechanical properties.

title: Physical Characteristics
point: Physical characteristics include electrical properties.

title: Physical Characteristics
point: Physical characteristics include chemical properties.

title: Physical Characteristics
point: Physical characteristics include biological properties.

title: Sensory Characteristics
point: Sensory characteristics include smell.

title: Sensory Characteristics
point: Sensory characteristics include touch.

title: Sensory Characteristics
point: Sensory characteristics include taste.

title: Sensory Characteristics
point: Sensory characteristics include sight.

title: Sensory Characteristics
point: Sensory characteristics include hearing.

title: Behavioral Characteristics
point: Behavioral characteristics include courtesy.

title: Behavioral Characteristics
point: Behavioral characteristics include honesty.

title: Behavioral Characteristics
point: Behavioral characteristics include veracity.

title: Temporal Characteristics
point: Temporal characteristics include punctuality.

title: Temporal Characteristics
point: Temporal characteristics include reliability.

title: Temporal Characteristics
point: Temporal characteristics include availability.

title: Ergonomic Characteristics
point: Ergonomic characteristics relate to physiological factors.

title: Ergonomic Characteristics
point: Ergonomic characteristics relate to human safety.

title: Functional Characteristics
point: Functional characteristics define operational performance.

title: Aircraft Example
point: Maximum speed of an aircraft is a functional characteristic.

title: Total Quality
point: Total Quality represents the way a company runs its business.

title: Total Quality Approach
point: Total Quality requires a structured approach.

title: Quality Improvement
point: Quality improvement is a major component of Total Quality.

title: Quality Control
point: Quality control is a major component of Total Quality.

title: Just-in-Time
point: Just-in-Time concept is part of Total Quality.

title: Business Improvement Activities
point: Total Quality includes other business improvement activities.

title: Organized Change
point: Total Quality mobilizes organized creation of beneficial change.

title: Cost Reduction
point: Continuous cost reduction is an outcome of Total Quality.

title: Variability Reduction
point: Elimination of variability is a key outcome of Total Quality.

title: Plant Downtime
point: Reduction in plant downtime is a benefit of Total Quality.

title: Yield Improvement
point: Increase in yield is a result of Total Quality.

title: JIT Outcome
point: Just-in-Time implementation is a Total Quality outcome.

title: Human Relations
point: Improved human relations result from Total Quality.

title: TQ vs Conventional Quality
point: Total Quality focuses on defect prevention.

title: Conventional Quality
point: Conventional quality often focuses on defect detection.

title: Prevention Philosophy
point: Defect prevention begins at the start of the process.

title: Detection Philosophy
point: Traditional systems detect defects at the end of production.

title: Cost of Non-Conformance
point: Total Quality relies on understanding the cost of non-conformance.

title: Universal Participation
point: Total Quality involves everybody at all levels.

title: All Stages Participation
point: Total Quality involves everyone at all stages of the process.

title: User Role
point: Every person acts as a user in quality performance.

title: Processor Role
point: Every person acts as a processor in quality performance.

title: Supplier Role
point: Every person acts as a supplier in quality performance.

title: Main Objective of TQ
point: Satisfying customer needs is the main objective of Total Quality.

title: Internal Customers
point: Total Quality considers internal customers important.

title: External Customers
point: Total Quality considers external customers important.

title: Customer Perception
point: Customer perception is the essence of Total Quality thinking.

title: Quality Improvement Team
point: Project teams apply Total Quality techniques for improvement.

title: Statistical Techniques
point: Total Quality uses statistical techniques.

title: Problem Solving
point: Problem-solving skills are essential in Total Quality.

title: Behavioral Concepts
point: Behavioral concepts support Total Quality implementation.

title: TQ Implementation
point: Introducing Total Quality alone is not sufficient.

title: Clear Objectives
point: Clear objectives must be known to all employees.

title: Teamwork
point: Teamwork provides real power to Total Quality.

title: Major Elements
point: Total Quality consists of four major elements.

title: Element 1
point: Systems is a major element of Total Quality.

title: Element 2
point: Processes is a major element of Total Quality.

title: Element 3
point: Management is a major element of Total Quality.

title: Element 4
point: People is a major element of Total Quality.

title: Systems Component
point: Quality audit belongs to systems.

title: Processes Component
point: Process capability belongs to processes.

title: Processes Component
point: Process control belongs to processes.

title: Management Component
point: Style belongs to management.

title: Management Component
point: Teamwork belongs to management.

title: Management Component
point: Cross-functional activity belongs to management.

title: Power House of TQ
point: People are the powerhouse of Total Quality.

title: Participation
point: People are managed through participation in improvement activities.

title: Voluntary Activities
point: Improvement activities should encourage voluntary participation.

title: People Component
point: Quality assurance belongs to the people element.

title: People Component
point: Process improvement belongs to the people element.

title: Seven Quality Tools
point: Total Quality uses seven quality management tools.

title: Tool 1
point: Pareto Analysis is a quality management tool.

title: Tool 2
point: Cause and Defect Diagram is a quality management tool.

title: Tool 3
point: Stratification is a quality management tool.

title: Tool 4
point: Check Sheet is a quality management tool.

title: Tool 5
point: Histogram is a quality management tool.

title: Tool 6
point: Scatter Diagram is a quality management tool.

title: Tool 7
point: Graph and Control Chart are quality management tools.

title: Tool Interaction
point: Interaction among quality tools makes Total Quality effective.

title: Policy Control
point: Organizations should strive for thorough policy control.

title: Priorities
point: Organizations should aim at priorities.

title: Problem Solving
point: Organizations should aim at problem solving.

title: Department Coordination
point: Coordinate quality

title: PDCA Cycle
point: Smooth PDCA cycles are essential.

title: PDCA
point: PDCA stands for Plan-Do-Check-Act.

title: Data Cycles
point: Use data cycles for fact finding.

title: Fact-Based Decisions
point: Fact finding is essential for quality improvement.

title: Supervisor Role 1
point: Study the existing cultural pattern.

title: Supervisor Role 2
point: Identify aspects that need change.

title: Supervisor Role 3
point: Secure active participation of others involved.

title: Customer Participation
point: Include customer participation in improvement activities.

title: Customer Assistance
point: Obtain active customer assistance.

title: Supervisor Role 4
point: Start quality initiatives on a small scale.

title: Gradual Expansion
point: Use results to broaden application.

title: Supervisor Role 5
point: Make quality improvement project by project.

title: Project Approach
point: Quality improvement should be project-based.

title: Supervisor Role 6
point: Use available quality tools.

title: Supervisor Role 7
point: Conduct studies using SQC tools.

title: SQC
point: Statistical Quality Control is abbreviated as SQC.

title: Tool Progression
point: Use simple to sophisticated SQC tools.

title: Supervisor Role 8
point: Provide time for mental change.

title: Mental Change
point: Behavioral change requires adequate time.

title: Supervisor Role 9
point: Carry out training programs as planned.

title: Supervisor Role 9
point: Carry out self-development programs as planned.

title: Supervisor Role 10
point: Avoid surprises during implementation.

title: Supervisor Role 11
point: Build quality into products during manufacturing.

title: Built-in Quality
point: Quality should be built into the process.

title: ISO
point: ISO stands for International Organization for Standardization.

title: ISO Nature
point: ISO is a world body.

title: ISO Membership
point: ISO is constituted by national standard-making organizations.

title: ISO Members
point: ISO includes BIS from India.

title: BIS
point: BIS stands for Bureau of Indian Standards.

title: ISO 9001
point: ISO 9001 is the model for a Quality Management System.

title: World Parity
point: ISO 9001 emerged from the need for world parity in standards.

title: Customer Requirements
point: ISO 9001 supports meeting customer requirements.

title: Indian Industry
point: ISO 9001 adoption is important for Indian industry.

title: European Market
point: European market functioning increased ISO significance.

title: Export Boost
point: Government export promotion increased ISO relevance.

title: Minimum Standards
point: ISO 9001 defines minimum quality standards.

title: International Acceptance
point: ISO 9001 enables international acceptability.

title: Certification Standard
point: ISO 9001 is the only certifiable standard in its family.

title: Certification Optional
point: Certification is not mandatory under ISO 9001.

title: Universal Applicability
point: ISO 9001 can be used by any organization.

title: Organization Size
point: ISO 9001 applies to large organizations.

title: Organization Size
point: ISO 9001 applies to small organizations.

title: Field Independence
point: ISO 9001 applies regardless of field of activity.

title: Global Adoption
point: More than one million organizations are ISO 9001 certified.

title: Country Coverage
point: ISO 9001 certification exists in over 170 countries.

title: Customer Focus
point: Strong customer focus is a basis of ISO 9001.

title: Top Management
point: Top management commitment is vital.

title: Process Approach
point: ISO 9001 promotes a process approach.

title: Continual Improvement
point: ISO 9001 promotes continual improvement.

title: Business Benefit
point: ISO 9001 helps provide consistent quality.

title: Product Quality
point: ISO 9001 supports good-quality products.

title: Service Quality
point: ISO 9001 supports good-quality services.

title: Objective of ISO 9001
point: Provide confidence in supplier capability.

title: Supplier Consistency
point: Suppliers should consistently provide products and services.

title: Customer Needs
point: Products must meet customer needs.

title: Customer Expectations
point: Products must meet customer expectations.

title: Regulatory Compliance
point: Products must comply with regulations.

title: Risk-Based Approach
point: ISO 9001 adopts a risk-based preventive approach.

title: Preventive Thinking
point: Risk-based thinking focuses on prevention.

title: Quality Coverage
point: Quality management covers top management commitment.

title: Customer Focus Coverage
point: Quality management covers customer focus.

title: Resource Adequacy
point: Quality management covers adequacy of resources.

title: Employee Competence
point: Quality management covers employee competence.

title: Process Management
point: Quality management covers process management.

title: Production Processes
point: Production processes are included in process management.

title: Service Delivery
point: Service delivery is covered by ISO 9001.

title: Administrative Processes
point: Administrative processes are included.

title: Support Processes
point: Support processes are included.

title: Quality Planning
point: Quality planning is covered by ISO 9001.

title: Design Control
point: Product and service design are covered.

title: Order Review
point: Incoming order review is covered.

title: Purchasing
point: Purchasing processes are covered.

title: Monitoring
point: Process monitoring is required.

title: Measurement
point: Measurement activities are required.

title: Conformity
point: Monitoring ensures conformity.

title: Complaint Handling
point: Processes must resolve customer complaints.

title: Corrective Action
point: Corrective actions are required.

title: Continuous Improvement
point: Improvement is a mandatory requirement.

title: Quality Management Principles
point: There are seven Quality Management Principles.

title: QMP 1
point: Customer-focused organization.

title: QMP 2
point: Leadership.

title: QMP 3
point: Engagement of people.

title: QMP 4
point: Process approach.

title: QMP 5
point: Improvement.

title: QMP 6
point: Evidence-based decision making.

title: QMP 7
point: Relationship management.

title: No Priority Order
point: Quality management principles are not prioritized.

title: Relative Importance
point: Importance varies among organizations.

title: Dynamic Importance
point: Importance may change over time.

title: ISO Certified Areas
point: All plants and units have ISO QMS certifications.

title: BSP Certification
point: BSP received Integrated Management System certification.

title: Certifying Agency
point: DNV is the certifying agency mentioned.

title: IMS
point: IMS stands for Integrated Management System.

title: IMS Component
point: QMS is integrated into IMS.

title: IMS Component
point: EMS is integrated into IMS.

title: IMS Component
point: OHSAS is integrated into IMS.

title: IMS Component
point: SAMS is integrated into IMS.

title: BSP Achievement
point: BSP became the first SAIL unit with this distinction.

title: Corporate Achievement
point: BSP is among few Indian corporate houses with this distinction.

title: SAIL Quality Policy
point: SAIL aims to build and sustain a world-class organization.

title: Quality Hallmark
point: Quality should be the hallmark of every process.

title: Quality Hallmark
point: Quality should be the hallmark of every activity.

title: Human Resource Involvement
point: Employee involvement is essential.

title: Dedication
point: Dedication of human resources is important.

title: Stakeholder Satisfaction
point: SAIL is committed to stakeholder satisfaction.

title: Innovation
point: Innovation supports stakeholder satisfaction.

title: Continual Improvement
point: Continual improvement supports stakeholder satisfaction.

title: TQ Structure
point: TQ = Improvement + Control + JIT + Business Improvement.

title: TQ Outcomes
point: Cost↓ Variability↓ Downtime↓ Yield↑ Relations↑.

title: Four Elements
point: Systems–Processes–Management–People.

title: Seven Tools
point: Pareto-Cause-Stratification-Check-Histogram-Scatter-Control Chart.

title: Seven Principles
point: Customer-Leadership-People-Process-Improvement-Evidence-Relationship.

title: TQ Focus
point: Total Quality focuses on prevention not detection.

title: ISO 9001 Purpose
point: ISO 9001 establishes a Quality Management System.

title: Key Benefit of ISO
point: Consistency in products and services.

title: Core Message
point: Customer satisfaction is the ultimate goal of Total Quality.
